Based on the provided context, the differences between Real Madrid and Barcelona (the team from Hostafrancs's city) are:

* **Match Wins:** Real Madrid leads the head-to-head results in competitive matches (95 wins to Barcelona's 91), while Barcelona leads in total matches (111 wins to Real Madrid's 99).
* **La Liga Titles:** Real Madrid has won the La Liga title a record 33 times, while Barcelona has won it 25 times (though from the 1990s onwards, Barcelona won 15 titles to Real Madrid's 9).
* **Financials and Valuation:** In the 2008–09 season, Real Madrid generated more revenue (€401 million) than Barcelona (€366 million). Real Madrid also ranked higher than Barcelona in Forbes' most valuable sports team lists in both 2010 and 2013.
* **UEFA Titles:** Real Madrid holds 12 European Cup/UEFA Champions League titles and 3 Intercontinental Cup wins. Barcelona holds 4 Cup Winners' Cup titles and shares the record for the most Super Cup wins (5).

required_n_flat_positive.note: required_n is the pooled >=3-hop cell size; power sized via the whole decide() rule (imported, not redefined). MATERIAL_F1_LIFT (0.02) sits at/above the implied pooled >=3-hop MDE at this N.

mde_at_full_corpus_n1165.note: PROPOSED ONLY — decide()/MATERIAL_F1_LIFT unchanged in this slice; HITL confirms the value at the next gate before a tiny decide() re-freeze.

projection.note: projected_full_pass_usd = required_N x #baseline_arms x per_call_usd. If required_N > corpus_answerable_ge3hop the power target is UNREACHABLE on this corpus for the +0.03 effect — HITL decision: raise the detectable effect size, accept the whole-corpus run at the stated sub-0.8 P(GO), or redirect (design §0 reserved follow-on 1-4).
